title: Non-Linear Returns

content.question: What's your 20% this month, and are you prioritizing it?
content.equation: Focus Ratio = % of Effort on Your Top 20% Activities
sop: List everything you did last month that drove a result: sales, content, outreach.
Rank them by actual output produced, not effort spent.
Calculate what percentage of your time went to the top-ranked 20% (see calculation, page 4).
Reallocate time away from the bottom 50% toward the top 20% this week.
Re-rank monthly, your highest-leverage 20% will shift as conditions change.
questions: What's your 20% this month, and are you prioritizing it?
Which single activity produced most of your results last quarter, and how much of your actual time went to it?
What are you still doing out of habit that, if you're honest, has never been part of your best outcomes?
content.equation_example: You spend 15% of your time on your top 20% highest-leverage activities. Focus Ratio = 15%. Higher — closer to what that top 20% could actually justify — means your effort is concentrated where it pays off; lower means most of your time goes to the low-leverage 80% that barely moves the needle.
content.closing_statement: Most results come from a small slice of effort.

caption:
Principle Nº 112: Non-Linear Returns

Effort rarely produces proportional results.

Ask yourself: What's your 20% this month, and are you prioritizing it?
